title: gumira thwaitesii
intro: Gumira thwaitesii, also known as the "Indian beech," is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It is native to Sri Lanka and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its expectorant and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Gumira thwaitesii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to leverage its health benefits and is sometimes used as a topical application to aid in wound healing.
